onboarding.question:
1. Resolve the open question on session TTL — confirm whether unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity with a 10-minute warning, or define the actual TTL and warning threshold the user will experience.
2. Define what "ambient awareness only" means in concrete, testable terms — specify whether the logged-out indicator is read-only text, contains no interactive elements, does not overlay other UI, and does not trigger any prompts or modals on its own.
onboarding.recommendations:
[1]. My recommendation: Unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity, with a warning surfaced to the user at 10 minutes remaining if they have at least one conversation exchange; the warning gives them sufficient time to complete SSO sign-up before session expiry.

→ Rationale: A 60-minute inactivity window is a standard balance between security and user experience; a 10-minute warning provides adequate lead time for SSO completion without excessive urgency; this threshold is already documented in the spec's edge cases and acceptance criteria.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[2]. My recommendation: "Ambient awareness only" means the logged-out indicator is read-only text that contains no interactive elements, no visual affordances (underlines, icons, highlights), does not overlay or occlude other UI, and does not trigger any prompts, modals, or sign-up suggestions on its own — it is purely informational.

→ Rationale: This definition operationalizes the minimalist design principle and aligns with the already-locked decision that the indicator is static text-only; it ensures the designer has a concrete, testable constraint that prevents the indicator from accidentally becoming a UI affordance.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

Once you approve these recommendations, I'll update the product spec to reflect each confirmed decision.
